input.initialPrompt: Before creating anything, show me exactly what files would be generated if I create a Blazor Web App called MyBlazorApp in ./test-output with interactive server rendering. Don't actually create the project yet.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Dry-run Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering

data.reasoningText:
The user wants to see what files would be generated by creating a Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering, without actually creating the project. Let me use the template-engine custom agent to handle this, as it's specialized for dotnet new operations.

Actually, let me think about this. The user wants a "dry run" - to see what files would be generated. I can use `dotnet new` with `--dry-run` flag to show what would be created without actually creating it.

Let me just run the command directly.

arguments.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "dotnet new blazor --name MyBlazorApp --output ./test-output --interactivity Server --dry-run 2>&1", "description": "Dry-run Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering", "initial_wait": 15}

toolArgs.description: Dry-run Blazor Web App with interactive server rendering

data.content:
Here's the dry-run output. The template would generate **68 files** in `./test-output/`:

**App core** (17 files):
- `Program.cs`, `MyBlazorApp.csproj`, `appsettings.json`, `appsettings.Development.json`
- `Properties/launchSettings.json`
- `Components/` — `App.razor`, `Routes.razor`, `_Imports.razor`
- `Components/Layout/` — `MainLayout`, `NavMenu`, `ReconnectModal` (`.razor`, `.razor.css`, some with `.razor.js`)
- `Components/Pages/` — `Counter`, `Error`, `Home`, `NotFound`, `Weather` (all `.razor`)

**Static assets** (51 files):
- `wwwroot/app.css`, `wwwroot/favicon.png`
- `wwwroot/lib/bootstrap/` — full Bootstrap CSS + JS distribution (minified, RTL variants, source maps)

A post-creation `dotnet restore` would also run automatically. Say the word when you're ready to create it!
